source_jobs.title: FDE
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.





THE ROLE

You'll be the technical architect behind customer success, designing and implementing zero-defect system configurations that translate messy, real-world operations into precise software logic.


This role is for engineers who want to see their work drive immediate business outcomes. Setup quality is the leading predictor of retention and expansion. Your technical decisions directly determine customer success.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Engineer zero-defect implementations: Deeply understand business logic and configure systems that integrate seamlessly with complex operations. Proactively solve edge cases before they become escalations. You may use Claude to automate workflows, but you should not expect to be coding on a day-to-day basis

 - Drive technical excellence: Write precise documentation, collaborate with engineering to surface product gaps, and inform roadmap decisions with real-world customer data.

 - Shape the category: You're joining early enough to define how technical operations scale and establish best practices from scratch.
   
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

Technical Foundation

 - BS/BA in Computer Science or related technical field

 - Strong programming fundamentals (Python, SQL, APIs, data structures)

 - You learn systems fast and architect elegant solutions to messy problems

Operator Mindset

 - You're a builder who wants to see immediate impact. You're energized by customer outcomes and business results, not just shipping features.

 - You thrive in ambiguity and love the challenge of translating complex real-world operations into technical solutions.

 - Obsessive attention to detail with zero tolerance for missed steps. You don't just follow processes, you optimize them.

 - High agency: you make things happen rather than waiting for them to happen.

Judgment & Communication

 - Exceptional written communication: you document with precision and clarity

 - Strong business acumen: comfortable discussing ROI, pricing logic, and operational workflows

 - You know when to solve problems independently, when to collaborate, and when to escalate



REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related technical field

 - 1-3 years in technical roles (exceptional new grads encouraged to apply)

 - NYC-based and excited about in-office collaboration in a high-intensity environment

 - Authorized to work in the United States



Why Join Probook?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir, ICPC world finalists, IMO Gold Medalists).

 - The Opportunity: You'll have direct impact on company trajectory, define technical operations from the ground up, and build systems that become the backbone of a category-defining company.
   
   
   


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity.

 - Salary: $105K – $135K

 -

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.



The Role

We have a clearly defined target: the top home service operators in America. The list exists. The product is proven. What we need now is someone to go get them.


You will own the top of the funnel for our enterprise AE team. Your job is to open doors through cold calls, cold emails, and LinkedIn outreach, qualify interest, and hand off high-quality opportunities to the AEs to close. You report to the Director of Growth and operate as the engine that keeps the pipeline full.



Who You Are

 - Relentless: You are comfortable making 60+ dials a day without being asked. You treat rejection as data, not discouragement. Your activity level is not negotiable with yourself.

 - Operator-to-operator communicator: You can talk to a plumbing business owner or an HVAC GM in their language. No jargon. No corporate SaaS pitch. You understand their world or learn it fast.

 - Highly organized: You run a clean CRM. You know your numbers cold. You never miss a follow-up.

 - Hungry to learn: You are early in your sales career and want to compress your development. You absorb coaching, apply it in the next call, and ask good questions.

 - Builder mentality: You thrive with no playbook. You figure out what works, document it, and do it at scale.



What you will own

 - Outbound pipeline: Build and execute a high-volume outbound motion across cold calls, email sequences, and LinkedIn targeting the top home service operators in the US.

 - Qualified opportunities: Deliver a consistent volume of qualified meetings to the AE team each month. Your primary metric is meetings held, not just booked.

 - Account research: Understand each target account before reaching out. Tailor your approach to their specific business size, software stack, and operational context.

 - CRM hygiene: Maintain clean, accurate records in Ashby for every prospect interaction. Your data is the team’s data.

 - Feedback loop: Surface patterns from outbound conversations to the Director of Growth and AE team. You are closer to the market than anyone. Use it.



Requirements

 - NYC Based: Excited to work in-office 5 days/week in our high-collaboration culture.

 - 1-2+ years of experience in a sales, SDR, or outbound-facing role

 - Prior cold calling experience

 - Exposure to B2B SaaS or vertical software a plus, not required

 - Exceptional verbal communication skills. You are direct, clear, and credible on the phone.

 - Strong written communication. Your emails are short, specific, and get replies.

 - High activity tolerance. You do not need external motivation to pick up the phone.

 - CRM discipline. You keep records clean without being told twice.



Why Join Probook?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir).



Compensation & Benefits

 - Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity

 - Base

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.




The Opportunity

This is not a role where you'll work on incremental features—you'll architect and ship the fundamental capabilities that define our platform.



Every feature you build will directly impact ARR. You'll see your work drive customer adoption in real-time, informed by direct customer feedback from our in-person onboarding process.



This is the ground floor of a category-defining company. The systems you build in the next 12 months will scale revenue.



What You'll Do

 - Build and deploy production AI systems that automate complex decision-making for thousands of service businesses

 - Ship features at a pace that would be quarterly initiatives at larger companies—expect weekend sprints

 - Work directly with customers to understand their workflows and translate needs into technical solutions

 - Integrate state-of-the-art AI frameworks and tools into production systems

 - Architect scalable backend infrastructure that supports rapid growth

 - Own critical product features end-to-end, from design through deployment



What We're Looking For

Technical Excellence

 - Exceptional coding fundamentals—comfortable with quant-level technical problems while leveraging AI coding tools as force multipliers

 - 2-6 years of backend engineering experience, ideally at fast-growing early-stage startups or as a founding engineer

 - Track record of building impressive systems or products (we actively evaluate for this in interviews)

 - Ability to master an existing codebase and ship meaningful contributions within 48 hours

Execution & Judgment

 - Extreme bias for action—you ship fast without sacrificing quality

 - Strong product sense and customer empathy—you understand what users need and how to deliver value

 - Architectural judgment—you build robust, scalable systems, not fragile quick fixes

 - Comfort with ambiguity and rapid context-switching



Why Join Now?

You're joining at the exact inflection point where the technical foundation is still being built — your architecture decisions will matter for years. The team is small enough that you'll shape company culture and technical standards.


In 12 months, this role will exist, but it won't be this role. Right now, you can still define everything.



Why Join Probook?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Cash-flow positive. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir, ICPC world finalists, IMO Gold Medalists).



Compensation & Benefits

 - Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity

 - Salary: $200K – $250K

 - Health: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision

 - Perks: $500 monthly Ramp card for commuting, meals, gym, etc. (plus $25 in nightly meal delivery credit) and really good office snacks

 - Access: Direct access to world-class investors and advisors

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.




The Opportunity

This isn't a role where you'll optimize existing features—you'll define and ship the product capabilities that unlock our next phase of hypergrowth. The products you build in the next 12 months will serve tens of thousands of users and process hundreds of millions in transactions.


You'll own entire product surfaces end-to-end, set technical and product quality standards for the engineering org, and work directly with leadership to translate business strategy into shipped product.


Every feature you ship will directly impact our ability to capture market share at speed. You'll see your work drive millions in ARR in real-time, validated by direct customer feedback from our in-person onboarding process.




What You'll Do

Product Ownership & Delivery

 - Own complete product surfaces from conception to production—backend systems, frontend interfaces, AI integrations, and everything in between

 - Ship production AI products that automate complex workflows for thousands of service businesses

 - Build features at a pace that would take quarters elsewhere—expect to move at startup velocity while maintaining staff-level quality

 - Make product and technical decisions that balance user needs, business impact, and technical excellence

 - Drive multi-month initiatives that unlock major product capabilities and revenue opportunities
   

Technical Leadership

 - Design systems and product architectures that scale to 100x current usage

 - Set the bar for product quality, technical craftsmanship, and user experience across the engineering org

 - Build robust, scalable systems that enable the team to iterate faster on top of your foundation

 - Integrate cutting-edge AI frameworks into customer-facing products, staying ahead of the rapidly evolving AI landscape

 - Make critical technical decisions about architecture, infrastructure, and tooling that impact company trajectory
   

Strategic Impact

 - Work directly with customers to understand workflows, identify pain points, and translate needs into shipped product

 - Partner with leadership to define product strategy and prioritize initiatives that drive ARR growth

 - Act as a force multiplier—your work should enable multiple engineers to ship better products faster

 - Mentor engineers and raise the bar through code reviews, product critiques, and technical leadership
   
   
   

What We're Looking For

Technical Excellence

 - 7+ years of product engineering experience shipping customer-facing products end-to-end

 - Deep expertise in building complete product experiences—you're comfortable across the stack and can ship features independently

 - Track record of building 0→1 products that reached meaningful scale, ideally as a founding or early engineer

 - Expert-level coding fundamentals with sophisticated judgment about technical trade-offs and where to invest quality

 - Battle-tested experience shipping production AI/ML products that customers depend on

 - Strong product instincts—you know how to build features that users actually want and that drive business outcomes
   

Product Sense & Execution

 - Exceptional product judgment—you understand what to build, how to build it, and when to ship it

 - Proven ability to make high-stakes product and technical decisions with incomplete information

 - Experience taking ambiguous customer problems and translating

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.


The Role

Enterprise Account Executives at Probook close deals by understanding the customer and the product and making both connect. You own the full sales cycle: discovery, evaluation, negotiation, and close. You are not a demo jockey. You are a problem solver who builds trust fast and earns the right to move fast with it.



You will have SDR support generating pipeline and marketing backing you in the field. In exchange, you are expected to manage your patch with precision, keep your process airtight, and hit your number. You will never sell what you would not buy. If you care about the craft of sales and want to sell a product customers actually love, this is the role.



Who You Are

 - You own the full sales cycle without needing to be managed through it. You are self-directed and process-driven by instinct, not instruction.

 - You understand complex workflow technology and can translate that into business value for operators who are not technical.

 - You build trust quickly. Your EQ is a competitive advantage in sales situations, not an afterthought.

 - You have operated in early-stage environments before. You know how to close with limited resources and incomplete information.

 - You are a deal-team player, not a lone wolf. You work closely with SDRs, Sales Engineers, and leadership to advance opportunities.

 - You are high-energy, gritty, and entrepreneurial. You take initiative and do not wait for permission.



What You Will Own

 - Account Planning: Know your patch better than anyone at the company. Map accounts that fit the ICP, identify key contacts, and develop and iterate your value hypothesis throughout each evaluation.

 - Pipeline Management: Use Probook's sales process to manage your pipeline with rigor. Understand the reality and risk of each deal based on stage exit criteria. When stuck, ask for help proactively.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with SDRs, Sales Engineers, Professional Services, and leadership to build, advance, and close opportunities. Deal team culture is central here.

 - Client Relationships: Own the relationship from initial engagement through close, renewal, and expansion. You are the constant.



Requirements

 - NYC Based: Excited to work in-office 5 days/week in our high-collaboration culture.

 - 5+ years of software sales closing experience, with demonstrated success selling complex workflow technology solutions.

 - No less than 1 year of enterprise sales experience.

 - Demonstrated intellectual capacity to understand underlying technology and articulate how it fits a customer's specific business needs.

 - Demonstrated EQ and relationship-building skills: the ability to get to trust quickly.

 - Proven grit and entrepreneurial spirit in early-stage or resource-constrained environments.

 - Comfortable with up to 10 to 20% domestic travel.




WHY JOIN PROBOOK?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming

source_jobs.title: Solutions Consultant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.




The Role

Probook is at an inflection point. We have product-market fit, a clearly defined target market, and capital to scale. What we need now is someone who can walk into a room with a skeptical operator and walk out with a champion.



As a Solutions Consultant, you are the technical and commercial bridge between what our product does and what our prospects need it to do. You own the demo, the proof of concept, and the ROI narrative. You work directly with AEs to advance and close deals, embedded in the deal from first call to signature. You will become the most knowledgeable person in the room on both the prospect's operations and Probook's capabilities. This is not a support role. You are a deal-closer with a technical edge.



Who You Are

 - A storyteller with substance. You run demos that make prospects see their own business differently. You don't recite features — you build narratives that land.

 - Operationally curious. You read a business fast. You ask the right questions, map their workflows, and identify where the pain lives before a prospect has finished explaining it.

 - Technically grounded. You understand software and systems well enough to credibly handle CTO and COO conversations in the same day — without pretending to be an engineer.

 - A deal-team player. You operate in sync with AEs, product, and engineering. You share context freely, close loops fast, and never let a deal stall because of a coordination gap.

 - Biased toward doing. You don't wait for a perfect demo environment or a complete asset library. You build what the deal needs and figure out the rest.



What You Will Own

 - Product demonstrations: Lead tailored demos for Probook's highest-value prospects. Own the narrative, adapt in real time to the room, and make every demo feel built specifically for that operator.

 - Proof of concepts: Build POCs that give prospects a real look at how Probook performs in their workflows. Own the ROI story — quantify outcomes in terms that matter to the buyer.

 - Deal strategy: Partner with AEs on account strategy, stakeholder mapping, and objection handling. Be the technical authority on every deal you touch.

 - Asset building: Work with Product, Product Marketing, and Engineering to create and maintain demos, whitepapers, RFP responses, and an internal knowledge base of customer use cases.

 - Trusted advisor relationship: Become the person prospects want in the room. Build confidence in both you and the product at every stage of the deal cycle.



Requirements

 - NYC Based: Excited to work in-office 5 days/week in our high-collaboration culture

 - 2+ years in a Solutions Consulting, Solutions Engineering, or Technical Consulting role — customer-facing, with a focus on B2B SaaS

 - Demonstrated ability to run product demos and build POCs that advance deals

 - Foundational understanding of software systems and system design — enough to hold technical conversations without being in the weeds

 - Strong written and verbal communication — you can simplify complexity without losing credibility

 - Comfortable with up to 10-20% domestic travel




Why Join Probook?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.



THE OPPORTUNITY

We're hiring a Business Operations Lead to work directly alongside the founders.



This is a broad, hands-on role at the center of a fast-moving company. You'll help keep the business running operationally - supporting financial models, maintaining reporting, running analyses, tracking cross-functional projects, and making sure things don't fall through the cracks. The scope is wide on purpose. You'll touch finance, operations, GTM, and product, and you'll have real visibility into how decisions get made from day one.



The founders own the strategy. Your job is to make them faster and better informed. If you want a clearly defined lane, this probably isn't the right fit. If you want to learn quickly, do real work early, and build operational instincts that most people spend years trying to develop, it's a genuinely rare opportunity.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

The work varies depending on what the business needs. Some days it's analytical, some days it's operational, some days it's just making sure the right things are happening. Across all of it, you'll be working closely with leadership and using good judgment about what to prioritize.


Day to day, that looks like:

 - Supporting the founders in building and maintaining financial models, forecasts, and operating plans

 - Building KPI dashboard and weekly metrics reporting so leadership always has a current, reliable view of the business

 - Running market, competitive, and customer analyses to support strategic decisions, packaged in a way that's easy to act on

 - Working across finance, operations, product, and GTM to help keep cross-functional initiatives moving

 - Helping evaluate new opportunities and partnerships by pulling data, modeling scenarios, and summarizing tradeoffs

 - Tracking special projects so they make progress without the founders managing the details

 - Identifying manual or repetitive workflows and building simpler systems to replace them

 - Using AI tools and automation to eliminate low-value work -- and actually building the solutions, not just spotting the opportunity




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We want someone who is analytically strong, reliable, and motivated to do what the business actually needs - not just the parts that look good on a resume.

 - 3+ years of experience in investment banking, consulting, strategic finance, or a similar high-output role

 - Prior startup experience is a strong plus, but we care more about how you work than where you've been.

 - Fluent in Excel and Google Sheets -- comfortable building things from scratch and working with data without a lot of hand-holding

 - A clear and organized writer whose work doesn't need significant editing before it goes to leadership

 - Comfortable operating when the direction isn't fully defined -- you make a reasonable call and keep moving

 - A genuine AI tools user who can show specific examples of how they use them to work faster

 - Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple workstreams without things slipping

 - Based in NYC and genuinely excited about working in-office five days a week




WHY PROBOOK

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18

source_jobs.title: Founding Design Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.


 


THE OPPORTUNITY

The home services industry runs on expertise built over decades, but the software these businesses use every day hasn't kept up. Most dispatchers are still working with tools that look like they were designed in 2008 - barely a step up from pen and paper - because for a long time, nobody with real product design chops was paying attention to this market.

That's the opportunity. Probook is the first product with the technical ambition to match what this industry actually deserves and you'll be the first designer to shape what it looks like. For a lot of the operators we work with, Probook is the most well-designed piece of software they've ever used in their professional lives. That bar is only going to get higher with you on the team.

You won't be optimizing a mature product or iterating on someone else's design system. You'll be defining what world-class looks like for an entire industry that's seeing it for the first time.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

Design Ownership & Delivery

 - Own the quality of Probook's UI/UX across our chrome extension, mobile product, and web platform end-to-end

 - Translate customer pain points from real conversations with real operators into product and UX improvements that ship

 - Obsessively explore the problem space and iterate before coming up with a solution. You know that the best product decisions come from a deep understanding of how operators work, not from settling on the first viable answer.

 - Independently ship front-end changes: you'll be making actual production code changes, not handing off to engineers and walking away

 - Move fast and sweat the details - you’re able to match the team’s velocity and turn things around in hours, not days/weeks without compromising on quality

Product Partnership

 - Work directly with engineers from spec to ship, closing the gap between what gets built and what should be built

 - Be a daily thought partner to the founders on product direction, with a real seat at the table on decisions that matter

 - Work directly with customers in the field to understand workflows and pain points. You can and should expect to be on calls with customers regularly to hear directly from them

 - Identify UX problems before customers surface them; anticipate common tradeoffs and design around them

Craft & Standards

 - Establish Probook's visual and interaction design language from the ground up

 - Use AI-assisted tools (Claude Code, Cursor, and similar) as force multipliers to spin up and iterate on prototypes at speed

 - Set the bar for design quality and detail orientation across everything the company ships

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Experience

 - 4–6 years of product design experience; the ideal trajectory is two serious stints (2+ years each), ideally at startups

 - has built bug-free frontend in production, doesn't need another engineer to make this happen

 - B2B SaaS background: you understand that enterprise and ops-focused product design is fundamentally different from consumer, and you've lived that difference

 - Strong portfolio of shipped work

Technical Fluency

 - A track record of shipping frontend code independently - not just handing off specs, but owning changes through to production

 - Fluency with AI-assisted development tools (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) is non-negotiable - we'll ask specifically about this

 - Can ta

source_jobs.title: Head of Talent
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.





ROLE DESCRIPTION

Probook is hiring a founding Head of Talent to build the recruiting engine for its next stage of growth. This person will own the systems, processes, and standards that make hiring repeatable at scale, while personally closing the company’s most important technical and leadership hires. The role spans intake discipline, scorecards, evaluation rubrics, ATS hygiene, hiring manager calibration, employer brand, and team development.


Probook needs a hands-on function builder who can protect a high talent bar under pressure, create engineering top-of-funnel from zero, and hire across engineering, product, sales, marketing, ops, and CS. This is a high-autonomy, CEO-reporting mandate for someone who has built an early-stage recruiting function before and is equally credible as a strategist, operator, and closer.




WHAT YOU WILL OWN

 - The Build: You will get under the hood of the existing process and add the structure that makes great hires repeatable as we scale. Intake discipline, scorecards, SLAs, evaluation rubrics, and ATS hygiene are all in scope.

 - The Hiring Engine: You will drive the hiring engine that helps us scale to meet our market opportunity spanning engineers, product, sales, marketing, ops, and CS, from IC to VP. You'll personally close the marquee hires while building the systems that let the broader team run the rest with confidence.

 - The Bar: You will protect Probook's quality bar across every function and level. You'll calibrate it across roles you've never personally hired for and push back on hiring managers who want to lower it, without sacrificing speed.

 - Brand From Zero: You will own engineering top-of-funnel, which is our biggest sourcing challenge. With minimal marketing budget, you'll build the content, community, and engineer-as-ambassador playbook that turns Probook into a destination for talent.

 - The Team: You will partner with our two existing recruiters and recruiting coordinator, develop them as the function grows, and expand the team over time as our hiring volume increases.




WHO YOU ARE

 - "A Function Builder": You've stood up or evolved a recruiting function at a venture-backed startup before 100 people. You've managed recruiters at depth, not just had them report to you. You have opinions about tooling, process, and what separates a great recruiter from a good one, and you can defend those opinions to the engineer or operator who pushes back.

 - "Hands-on Closer": You'd rather close a critical hire yourself than route them through a coordinator. You've personally landed critical hires, run searches, or owned the first 50 to 80 hires at a startup solo. "I usually manage the process, not execute it" is not in your vocabulary.

 - "Bar Defender": You've actually pushed back on a founder or VP who wanted to move a candidate forward that didn’t hit the bar, and you can tell the story. You calibrate by going deep on prior best hires, not by reading a JD. Under velocity pressure, you don't fold; you offer alternatives.

 - "0-to-1 Brand Builder": You've built an employer brand at a company that didn't yet have one. You've organized meetups, run dinners that converted into hires, and convinced engineers to spend time on content without burning their week. You view a brand as a top-of-funnel multiplier, not a marketing line item.

 - "Cross-Functional": You've hired engin

source_jobs.title: Strategic Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.


The Role

Strategic Account Executives at Probook close deals by understanding the customer and the product and making both connect. You own the full sales cycle: discovery, evaluation, negotiation, and close. You are not a demo jockey. You are a problem solver who builds trust fast and earns the right to move fast with it.



You will have SDR support generating pipeline and marketing backing you in the field. In exchange, you are expected to manage your patch with precision, keep your process airtight, and hit your number. You will never sell what you would not buy. If you care about the craft of sales and want to sell a product customers actually love, this is the role.



Who You Are

 - You own the full sales cycle without needing to be managed through it. You are self-directed and process-driven by instinct, not instruction.

 - You understand complex workflow technology and can translate that into business value for operators who are not technical.

 - You build trust quickly. Your EQ is a competitive advantage in sales situations, not an afterthought.

 - You have operated in early-stage environments before. You know how to close with limited resources and incomplete information.

 - You are a deal-team player, not a lone wolf. You work closely with SDRs, Sales Engineers, and leadership to advance opportunities.

 - You are high-energy, gritty, and entrepreneurial. You take initiative and do not wait for permission.



What You Will Own

 - Account Planning: Know your patch better than anyone at the company. Map accounts that fit the ICP, identify key contacts, and develop and iterate your value hypothesis throughout each evaluation.

 - Pipeline Management: Use Probook's sales process to manage your pipeline with rigor. Understand the reality and risk of each deal based on stage exit criteria. When stuck, ask for help proactively.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with SDRs, Sales Engineers, Professional Services, and leadership to build, advance, and close opportunities. Deal team culture is central here.

 - Client Relationships: Own the relationship from initial engagement through close, renewal, and expansion. You are the constant.



Requirements

 - NYC Based: Excited to work in-office 5 days/week in our high-collaboration culture.

 - 10+ years of software sales closing experience, with demonstrated success selling complex workflow technology solutions.

 - No less than 5 years of enterprise sales experience.

 - Demonstrated intellectual capacity to understand underlying technology and articulate how it fits a customer's specific business needs.

 - Demonstrated EQ and relationship-building skills: the ability to get to trust quickly.

 - Proven grit and entrepreneurial spirit in early-stage or resource-constrained environments.

 - Comfortable with up to 10 to 20% domestic travel.




WHY JOIN PROBOOK?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Cit

source_jobs.title: Founding Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.




THE ROLE
You'll sit front row as the right hand to 3 founders building one of the fastest-growing startups in NYC, backed by two tier-1 VCs. This is a high-ownership, high-trust role built for someone exceptional at this craft.


You will support three senior executives across calendar management, communications, travel, cross-executive coordination, and operational reliability. You will be in the room for sensitive conversations, managing competing priorities in real time, and making judgment calls that keep three leaders moving at the pace this business demands.



WHO YOU ARE

 - You have supported multiple senior executives at the same time and have specific stories to prove it.

 - Nothing falls through the cracks on your watch.

 - You see two steps ahead, already preparing for what hasn’t been asked yet.

 - You write clearly and professionally, and you calibrate quickly.

 - You are discreet. You handle sensitive information with maturity and zero leakage.

 - You are not here on the way to something else. You are here because you are good at this and you want to master it.
   
   

WHAT YOU WILL OWN

 - Executive time protection: Own founders’ calendars completely. Filter and triage requests, deliver meeting prep 24 hours in advance, and surface conflicts before they happen.

 - Communications: Manage all inboxes. Triage, draft, route, and follow up. Priority items get a same-day response. Nothing drops.

 - Travel and logistics: Own all travel end-to-end; flights, hotels, ground transport, itineraries, contingencies. Fix disruptions before the exec finds out. Process expenses within 5 business days.

 - Cross-executive coordination: Keep Founders aligned on shared commitments. Own board meetings, all-hands, and investor events end-to-end. Route information between the three that would otherwise get lost.

 - Office and operational reliability: Own vendors, office systems, and recurring workflows. Run team events, off-sites, and exec-level onboarding logistics.
   
   

REQUIREMENTS

 - NYC-based. In-office 5 days/week. This is not negotiable.

 - 2-5 years of experience in a related role.

 - Start-up experience is a great plus, but not required.

 - Demonstrated experience supporting more than one senior executive simultaneously.

 - Exceptional written communication — you will represent three executives in written form.

 - Comfortable with fast-moving, high-standard personalities. Probook moves fast, and the founders are demanding. This is expected, not exceptional.

 - Track record of taking ownership and delivering without being micromanaged.

 - Startup experience strongly preferred. Not required if the candidate shows exceptional judgment and pace.
   
   

WHY JOIN PROBOOK?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live in the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir).
   
   

COMPENSATIO

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry, transforming how plumbing, HVAC, and electrical businesses operate.

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.


THE OPPORTUNITY

The Deployment Strategist owns the post-sale technical and operational journey for new customers — from initial configuration through go-live to early adoption. This is a project-ownership role: you are responsible for hitting deployment milestones and go-live dates, not just holding a relationship. You sit between the sales handoff and the customer success team, and you are the person accountable for a customer's first experience actually running on the product.

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Run kickoff calls and set expectations with new customers on scope, timeline, and what a successful deployment looks like

 - Own the end-to-end configuration and setup of the product for each new account

 - Build and manage a deployment project plan, keeping timelines, stakeholders, and internal teams aligned to hit go-live dates

 - Present deck overviews and progress updates to customer stakeholders, from operators to executives

 - Troubleshoot and resolve technical and workflow issues that come up during implementation, without routing them elsewhere

 - Train end users and ensure the product is actually being adopted, not just installed

 - Identify and flag risk early when a deployment is falling behind, or a customer is disengaged

 - Hand off cleanly to the Customer Success team once a customer is live and stable

 - Provide feedback to product and engineering based on patterns seen across deployments

 

WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2+ years in an implementation, deployment, or onboarding role at a SaaS company (title may vary: Implementation Specialist, Onboarding Manager, Deployment Strategist, Technical Onboarding Manager)

 - Direct, hands-on experience running the full implementation lifecycle for a software product — not just supporting one

 - Comfortable running structured kickoff calls, presenting to stakeholders, and managing a project plan independently

 - Track record of hitting deployment deadlines and can speak to specifics (number of implementations run, average time-to-go-live, customer count)

 - Strong ownership instinct — when something breaks, or a customer is stuck, you solve it yourself rather than escalating

 - Comfortable learning a technically complex product quickly and becoming the go-to expert on it

 - Strong written and verbal communication; able to translate technical detail into plain language for non-technical stakeholders

 - Organized and deadline-driven; able to run multiple concurrent deployments without dropping details

 - Based in or willing to relocate to the NYC area; this is a five-day-a-week in-office role
   

WHY PROBOOK

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir).

 


COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity.

 - Salary: $110,000 - $130,000 (Can flex depending on YOE)

 - Health:

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PROBOOK

Probook is the AI Operating System for the home services industry and is backed by two of the top VC's in the world (Sequoia & a16z)

 

Our impact is real. Dispatching the right technician to the right job can mean the difference between a $300 "band-aid" fix and a $20,000 system replacement. By automating previously high-touch workflows around-the-clock, Probook ensures a consistent customer experience without the need for 24/7 live staffing. We unify our customers' processes into a single, scalable platform, giving businesses one place to run their entire dispatch operation.





The Role

You will carry a portfolio of Probook's highest-ACV accounts and own their success — from post-deployment through renewal and expansion. You drive outcomes, not just relationships. GRR, NRR, and expansion are your numbers to hit.




Who You Are

 - An operator first: You run your book like a business. You track health scores, flag churn risk before it surfaces, and hold customers accountable to the outcomes they signed up for.

 - Commercially sharp: You spot expansion opportunities naturally, build airtight business cases, and are comfortable asking for the renewal and the upsell in the same conversation.

 - A strong executor: You manage a portfolio of complex, multi-stakeholder accounts without dropping anything. Your project management and follow-through skills are airtight.

 - Direct and confident: You communicate clearly and concisely with executives, operators, and economic buyers alike. You deliver hard messages without hedging and earn trust through follow-through, not warmth.

 - Low ego, high output: No task is beneath you. You do whatever it takes to make your accounts successful.




What You Will Own

 - A portfolio of Probook's largest and most strategic accounts, with full ownership of GRR, NRR, and expansion targets

 - The full post-launch relationship: Go-Live through renewal, including QBRs, health checks, escalation management, and expansion plays

 - Cross-functional accountability: work directly with Deployment, Product, and Leadership to surface customer feedback, resolve blockers, and close product gaps that affect retention




Requirements

 - NYC-based

 - 5-7+ years in B2B SaaS Customer Success with a 3M+ book of business

 - Startup experience is needed unless you can prove you have built playbooks 0-1 (Series A–D)

 - Demonstrated ownership of enterprise/strategic accounts (10-30 clients max)

 - Experience managing multi-stakeholder accounts with 3+ decision makers or business units

 - Vertical SaaS OR field operations software OR Home services industry experience is a plus, though by no means required
   
   
   

Why Join Probook?

 - The Tailwinds: $700B industry with clear tailwinds: AI adoption, labor shortages, and generational transitions creating demand for operational modernization. We are defining the category, not fighting for share.

 - The Traction: $XXM ARR in 18 months. Product-market fit proven; now we're scaling.

 - Funding: $40M raised from Sequoia and Andreessen Horowitz

 - The Team: >60 people, our founding team comes from the space and has spent thousands of days onsite with our customers. We live the space. We're also becoming one of the most talent-dense start-ups in NYC (MIT, Harvard, UPenn M&T, Waterloo, Citadel, Palantir).

 - The Greenfield Ownership: You own the outcomes, not just the relationships. And we have a lot of strategic accounts that need owning.




Compensation & Benefits

 - Equity: Meaningful early-stage equity

 - Base Salary: $160,000 - $200,000 OTE

 - Health: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision

 - Perks: $500 monthly Ramp card for commuting, meals, gym, etc. (plus $25 in nightly meal delivery credit) and really good office snacks

 - Access: Direct access to world-class investors and advisors

notes: Probook is an AI Operating System for the home services industry, with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
